title: One Tray Chicken & Fondant Potatoes
summary: Golden, crispy-skinned chicken baked over soft, stock-soaked potatoes with lemon, rosemary and sweet onion, all in one tray so you can have a delicious hands off dinner.

ingredients.label: 4 Large Chicken Thighs, bone in, skin on
ingredients.name: Bone-In Skin-On Chicken Thigh

ingredients.label: 4 Large Chicken Thighs, bone in, skin on
ingredients.name: Chicken

method.description: Heat the oven to 200°C. Season the chicken thighs with salt and pepper. Place skin-side down in a deep, heavy-bottomed casserole dish or ovenproof pan. Set over a medium heat and cook for around 10 mins until the skin is golden and crisp, then flip and cook for another 2 mins.

Meanwhile, halve the potatoes. Working in batches, nestle as many potatoes as you can (cut-side down) into the space around the chicken to brown in the chicken fat for 2 mins. Remove and repeat with the rest. Peel the onion and cut into 6 wedges, also browning in the pan as the chicken cooks.
Smash the garlic cloves. Strip the rosemary leaves from their stalks and roughly chop.

Push the chicken to one side of the pan, Shuffle the potatoes to cover the bottom of the potatoes, scatter over the garlic and rosemary, and season everything with salt and black pepper, then place the chicken on top (skin side up). Cut the lemon in half and tuck it into the pan. Pour over the chicken stock.
Transfer the pan to the oven and roast for 30 mins, until the chicken is cooked through and the potatoes are tender.
Lift the chicken out onto a plate to rest. Put the pan back on a high heat and simmer to reduce the stock slightly.
Shave the parmesan over a bowl of rocket and toss with a little oil. Spoon everything into bowls and pour over any remaining pan juices and serve with the salad. .

chefs.name: Anna Helm Baxter
chefs.summary: Anna Helm Baxter is a cookbook author, food stylist, and food producer at Mob. Having spent most of her life in the US, she loves potlucks, tailgates, and a good Thanksgiving spread. As a working mum of three, she gets the dinner struggle, which is why she’s all about recipes that are joyful, healthy, and totally doable. And if all else fails she always has cookies stashed in the freezer.
